mnemonics.title: Java Token Types
mnemonics.content: KILO: Keywords, Identifiers, Literals, Operators (separators are the 5th)

mnemonics.title: Separators Remember
mnemonics.content: Parentheses, Braces, Brackets: () {} [] then semicolon and comma: ; , . - The 9 separators.

mnemonics.title: Identifier Rules
mnemonics.content: Letters, Digits, $, _ (but cannot START with digit, cannot be keyword)

acronyms.usage: Five main token categories in Java (excluding separators and comments)

acronyms.expansion: American Standard Code for Information Interchange
acronyms.usage: Character encoding standard where Java chars extend from 8-bit to 16-bit Unicode
Executes bytecode; lexical analysis happens before JVM execution

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a scanner reading left-to-right, grouping characters into meaningful chunks (tokens) like words in English
Think of char as a 16-bit box holding values 0-65535, each representing a unique character symbol
\n is not two characters - it's one newline control character represented by backslash-n

analogies.analogy: Like words in a sentence - each has meaning and purpose, separated by spaces (whitespace)
Like names for people - must follow naming rules, cannot be reserved names like 'Mr.' or 'Mrs.'
Character is a single person ('A'), String is a group/team ("ABC") - different data types
